subject: Geography
title: Maps in Augmented Reality
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Maps in Augmented Reality.

hook.text: AR glasses overlay map data onto the real world—directions float in your field of vision.

buildup.text: Google's Live View already lets you see walking directions pinned onto real streets.

discovery.text: Surgeons use AR maps of the human body to navigate during operations.

twist.text: AR could make paper and screen maps obsolete—the world itself becomes the map.

climax.text: When maps are invisible layers over reality, the line between map and world disappears.

punchline.text: The best map might be one you see through, not look at.

quiz.question: How does AR change the concept of maps?
options: It overlays map data directly onto the real world
It creates physical map prints faster
